title: hemionitis glauca
intro: Hemionitis glauca, also known as the silver spleenwort or glaucous hemionitis, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. Hemionitis glauca can be prepared in different herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, or infusions, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
intro_study: According to "Journal of the Indian Medical Association", hemionitis glauca, as part of a herbal eye drop preparation, was found to improve vision in a few cases of cataract.
